document.source_title: New heart health diet: Food discipline from childhood, cut sugar and don’t start on alcohol at all

claims.claim_text: The AHA guidelines recommend starting dietary changes early.
claims.evidence_text: Though the AHA doesn’t mention how early, probably factoring in those on poor, energy-deficient diets, it can be assumed that healthy diet discipline is crucial for foundational years.

claims.claim_text: Alcohol consumption is discouraged in the new guidelines.
claims.evidence_text: Don’t get started on alcohol if you haven’t. It’s poison for your heart and weakens its muscles from the very first sip.

claims.claim_text: The guidelines emphasize a holistic approach to heart health.
claims.evidence_text: It takes a bold stance against alcohol consumption, calling for immediate limits by drinkers, and links a good diet with exercise and weight.

entities.name: American Heart Association
entities.description: AHA is responsible for issuing the new heart health diet guidelines.
entities.evidence_text: These are guidelines for a heart-friendly diet issued by the American Heart Association.

entities.name: Circulation
entities.description: The journal where the new guidelines were published.
entities.evidence_text: which have been published in the journal ‘Circulation.’

entities.name: Dr Ranjan Shetty
entities.description: Lead cardiologist and medical director at Sparsh Hospital, Bengaluru.
entities.evidence_text: says Dr Ranjan Shetty, lead cardiologist and medical director at Sparsh Hospital, Bengaluru.

metrics.name: added sugar intake
metrics.evidence_text: It has been estimated that adults consuming above 25% of energy from added sugars have a nearly three-fold higher risk of CVD mortality compared with those consuming less than 10% of energy from added sugars.

citations.title: New heart health diet: Food discipline from childhood, cut sugar and don’t start on alcohol at all

source_credibility.reason: The source is a reputable news outlet reporting on guidelines from a recognized health organization.

geolocation.name: Bengaluru
geolocation.evidence_text: says Dr Ranjan Shetty, lead cardiologist and medical director at Sparsh Hospital, Bengaluru.
